Report: Pre Professional 
​
​
​
​
 
 
Facultad de Ingeniería y Ciencias Aplicadas,  
Universidad de los Andes  
 
Sofia Acosta  
Degree: 5th year  
 
FORUS S.A 
Retail Business 
___________________________________________________________________________ 
 
Abstract 
During my internship at Forus S.A., I worked in the Sustainability Sub-Management under 
the supervision of Francisca Menichetti Pilasi. My main responsibilities included contributing 
to the 2024 Annual Report, focusing on sustainability, and developing a database to classify 
sustainable product attributes across the company’s brand portfolio. 
The Annual Report task required extensive research, data collection, and coordination with 
brand managers to ensure accurate reporting of sustainability initiatives. I also participated 
in meetings with external agents to optimize compliance with the Extended Producer 
Responsibility (REP) Law, reinforcing Forus’s commitment to environmental sustainability. 
One of the biggest challenges was building a database to identify sustainable products. This 
process involved organizing more than 7,600 SKUs and 109 columns in Excel, using dynamic 
tables, conditional formatting, and advanced filters. This tool will facilitate the tracking of 
sustainable products and help Forus adapt to future regulations. 
Additionally, the internship allowed me to gain insights into retail operations, from in-store 
activities to sustainability management. I collaborated with Investor Relations and 
Sustainability teams, strengthening my communication and project management skills. 
The internship experience was essential for my professional growth, enhancing my technical 
skills, fostering personal development, and providing valuable insights into the operations of 
a large company. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

1. Introduction 
1.1 Company Description  
 
Forus S.A. is a company in the retail sector that specialize in footwear, clothing, and 
accessories for women, men, youth, and children. It was founded in August 1980 and began 
its operations as Hush Puppies Chile Ltda. Through the years, the brand portfolio and 
operations of the organization have expanded, building a strong position in the design, 
development, and distribution of footwear, apparel, and accessories. Currently Forus is 
operating in four countries: Chile, Peru, Colombia, and Uruguay, successfully integrating 
both international and in-house brands into its portfolio. 
This organization distributes 28 prestigious international and proprietary brands, including 
Columbia, Under Armour, Vans, Hush Puppies, Rockford, among others. These brands cover 
multiple sections including outdoor, action sports, athletic wear, lifestyle, and safety 
footwear. The company operates under a phygital ecosystem that effectively integrates 
physical stores, proprietary e-commerce websites, and marketplaces. This approach ensures 
a superior customer experience across all channels. 
Image 1: General diagram of the company 
This image shows the company's structure, which is led by the Board of Directors and 
General Management, with key areas such as Finance, Sustainability, Logistics, and Digital, 
as well as regional management in Colombia, Uruguay, and Peru. In addition, there is a 
close relationship between the different areas of the company, which is essential for 
optimizing operational efficiency and fostering collaboration among teams. 
The company's long journey has led to an organization with 3,421 employees. This large 
team reflects Forus commitment to growth and continuous development. Furthermore, total 
sales in 2023 reached 326,555 million pesos, supported by a strong network of sales 
channels that includes 399 physical stores, 46 e-commerce websites, and participation in 13 
marketplaces. 
 

1.2 Area Description  
In 2023, Forus created a Sustainability Sub Management, led by my supervisor, Francisca 
Menichetti Pilasi. The mission of this area is to identify the environmental, social, and 
governance (ESG) issues that have the greatest impact on the company, its stakeholders, 
and the environment, with the goal of focusing efforts and resources on initiatives that allow 
for progress and the monitoring of these key aspects. 
The Sustainability department integrates sustainable practices into all operations of the 
company, standing out for implementing circular economy projects and waste management, 
such as the “Paperless” program and compliance with the REP law (Extended Producer 
Responsibility), where I worked on some projects related to it. It also develops sustainability 
indicators for products and leads initiatives like 'For the Community,' promoting corporate 
volunteering and activities with a positive social and environmental impact 
This department reports directly to the Legal and People Management, which allows it to 
address sustainability issues from a corporate perspective and work on them strategically. 
Additionally, it collaborates closely with the Investor Relations Management, ensuring that 
sustainability initiatives align with stakeholder expectations and that progress on ESG 
matters is effectively communicated. 
During my internship, I participated in various projects, the most important being the 
preparation of the company's Annual Report 2024, with a special focus on the sustainability 
section. To develop this task, it was necessary to do a lot of research and also coordinate 
meetings with all the company’s brands, which allowed me to work directly with the brand 
managers to gather information about their sustainable initiatives. 
Additionally, I worked closely with Martina Avsolomovich, an intern of Isabel Darrigrandi, 
Head of Investor Relations (as Francisca and Isabel were responsible for drafting the 
document of the Annual Report). I also developed an extensive database to classify and 
track sustainable attributes across all the brand's products. This task allowed a more 
comprehensive understanding of their sustainable offerings, helping the company to identify 
environmentally friendly products more effectively.  
Image 2: Sustainability Department diagram 
My supervisor, Francisca Menichetti, had a direct relationship with me since she is the 
Sub-Manager and she is the only one in the department. She works closely with her intern, 
which allowed me to learn about various topics, especially sustainability strategies in a retail 
company and interaction with other departments. 
 
 

2. Work Details  
2.1 General Activities 
As mentioned earlier, a large part of my work was focused on gathering information for the 
2024 Annual Report, specifically in the sustainability section. To carry out this task, it was 
first necessary to collect and analyze a significant amount of information from the 
sustainability reports of the various Forus brands. 
The first days of my internship were dedicated to reading and analyzing the company’s 2023 
Annual Report, which involved several hours of work and research. This task was essential 
as it allowed me to familiarize myself with environmental concepts I was unfamiliar with and 
gain a deeper understanding of the structure of Chapter 6, titled "Our Planet and 
Communities," which was the chapter I had to develop for the 2024 Annual Report. 
Image 3: Organization of Annual Report document   
To begin this task, documents were created for each chapter of the report so they could be 
updated. As I mentioned earlier, I worked on chapter 6 and organized the document, as can 
be seen in image 3.  
Image 4: Document Structure 
Then I started gathering information for the 2024 Annual Report, where I created a 
well-structured document with tables for each brand, featuring two main columns: 2023 and 
 

2024. In the first column, I wrote the information available in the 2023 Annual Report, while 
in the second, I added only the relevant updates for each topic. This required extensive 
research on the various brands in the Forus portfolio, with a big focus on Crocs and 
L'Occitane, as they were incorporated that same year and lacked prior information in the 
report. For brands like Columbia, Rockford, VF Company, and Under Armour, the task was to 
update the existing data, as can be seen in the image 4 
However, in some cases, the sustainability reports had not yet been published, as each 
company has its own reporting schedule. In these situations, it was necessary to 
communicate directly with the brands to obtain updated information for the 2024 fiscal year. 
As part of this task, I drafted emails in English and coordinated meetings with the managers 
of each brand, to validate and complement the data. These meetings also provided key 
information on initiatives that were more specific and couldn't be found in sustainability 
reports. 
Additionally, during my internship, I participated in weekly meetings with external agents 
(Kyklos) specialized in the REP Law. The objective of these meetings was to improve the 
automation of the information required to comply with the packaging regulation. Previously, 
the company was required to submit information on the REP Law once a year, but now it 
must do so monthly. Due to this change in reporting frequency, the company is seeking to 
automate the process. 
On the other hand, a few days before Christmas, the interns were called in to support the 
Forus stores at Alto Las Condes Mall. This experience allowed me to gain a deeper 
understanding of how the brands operate, particularly in terms of sales, inventory 
management, and distribution. Additionally, I had the opportunity to observe how the REP 
Law is implemented in malls, particularly in relation to the waste management of boxes. 
Lastly, during the final weeks of my internship, I focused on creating an extensive database 
for Francisca. This database consisted of collecting and organizing information from the 
catalogs of footwear, apparel, and accessories for Summer 2024 from all Forus brands. 
My main task was classifying sustainable products, which involved determining the 
sustainable attributes that could be assigned to each brand. For example, in the case of the 
Merrell brand, some footwear contained recycled polyester, which allowed them to be 
classified within the sustainable product category. To do this, it was necessary to analyze the 
specifications of each product in detail and structure the information in a way that made it 
easy to identify sustainability attributes within the catalog. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

2.2 Most Challenge Activity 
 
The most challenging task at Forus was creating a database to classify the sustainable 
attributes of each brand. This was particularly demanding due to my limited familiarity with 
advanced Excel tools. Consequently, I dedicated time to researching and exploring various 
functions to improve my skills, ultimately achieving an efficient and precise classification of 
the attributes assigned to each brand. 
 
The creation of this database was as follows: I individually downloaded the product catalogs 
from each brand and then consolidated them into a single Excel file to generate a 
comprehensive database for the company. 
 
The database contains approximately 7,600 SKUs (unique product identification codes) 
corresponding to the summer season of 2024, including all products sold in late 2024 and 
early 2025. In total, this excel had 109 columns, where each one represents different 
attributes of the product, such as brand, code, model, class, season, among others. 
 
Image 5: Screenshot database  
The main issue of this task and the reason it was assigned was the lack of accurate 
classification of sustainability products within the product catalog of each brand. As I 
mentioned earlier, this catalog contained 109 columns, being one of these columns called 
“Sustainable product”. A few brands completed this column correctly, and in many other 
cases, the information admitted was exactly or incomplete as it can be appreciated in image 
5.  
The lack of proper categorization impedes the identification and analysis of products with 
sustainable attributes, requiring a more detailed review and classification process. In 
addition, it is anticipated that in the coming years, Chile will include textiles as a priority 
product under the REP Law, which will require companies to implement waste management 
systems and take responsibility for the entire lifecycle of their products.  
The idea of creating this database was to make dynamic tables in Excel, which will help 
Francisca organize and analyze the data more easily in the future. These dynamic tables will 
allow her to filter and summarize the information based on the sustainable attributes of the 
products, making the analysis process much more efficient.  
Continuing, I developed the methodology for classifying attributes in different steps. First, I 
added two new columns to the database: “Attributes”, where I specified the characteristics 
 

that considered a product sustainable, and “Master Column”, which indicated the specific 
Excel column containing the relevant information. This structure facilitated efficient 
organization and retrieval of data pertinent to the classification of sustainable products. 
Image 6: New columns added 
Then, to classify which attribute would be considered, I began to realize a deep analysis of 
each brand individually. Over time, I created a “dictionary” of sustainable terms, allowing me 
to automate and organize the product classification process, as illustrated in the following 
image: 
Image 7: Dictionary of sustainable terms 
I created this dictionary to organize all the attributes, making them easy to find. In the first 
column, the brands are listed, the second column contains the assigned attribute, and the 
third column indicates in which catalog column the attribute can be found. 
To identify these terms, I utilized various Excel tools. Initially, I made numerous mistakes 
due to my limited knowledge of the software. However, through trial and error, I gradually 
optimized the process.  
 

Utilizing Excel tools such as Text to Columns, Conditional Formatting, and Advanced Filters 
simplified the process of identifying attributes within the 7,600 SKU, improving the efficiency 
of my research. 
Image 8: Conditional Formatting 
In image 8, it can be seen how the tool of conditional formatting works. This tool highlights 
cells containing a specific word; for example, in the illustration, it highlights if it says 
“recycled”. I used keywords related to sustainability, such as "recycled," "organic," "certified 
leather," "ecological.", among others. I also incorporated brand specific terms like Bluesign 
(a sustainability certification) or Repreve (a recycled polyester fiber made from used plastic 
bottles). 
After completing the dictionary, I utilized this tool to navigate to the two new columns I had 
created in the database. I organized the data by brand and systematically input information 
for each sustainable product. For instance, in Image 6, if a product featured a "100% 
recycled polyester lining," this attribute was classified as sustainable, and the corresponding 
column in the Excel sheet where this information could be found was indicated. To do this 
task I used Advanced Filters to be more organized.  
One of the main challenges I faced was that, on some occasions, the same product had 
multiple sustainable attributes. For example, a shoe was made from recycled fibers and also 
had a sole made from BLOOM, a material derived from algae. Since the purpose of this 
database is to analyze the data using dynamic tables, it was not possible to write both 
"recycled fibers" and "BLOOM sole" in the same cell. Doing so would have messed up the 
organization and stopped the dynamic tables from working correctly. Because of this, 
whenever I faced this issue, I had to create a new row in the database. I would input all the 
product information again, only changing the attribute and the master column. For example, 
in Image 9, it can be seen a SKU with different attributes. While the product code and all 
other information remain the same, the only change is in the columns I created. 
Image 9: Same product with different attributes 
 

This task taught me the importance of attention to detail because to complete this task 
effectively, it was crucial to maintain full concentration at all times. Given the large volume 
of rows and columns in the database, it was very easy to make mistakes, such as 
accidentally adding or deleting a row, which could have led to errors in the entire dataset. 
The sheer size of the database meant that even a small oversight could affect the accuracy 
of the information, making attention to detail and careful organization essential throughout 
the process. 
In conclusion, my work on the database gave Forus a solid foundation to manage its 
sustainable products effectively. This will not only help the company stay organized but also 
prepare for future regulations such as the REP Law. By accurately tracking sustainable 
attributes, Forus can meet the legal requirements and maintain transparency with 
consumers and stakeholders. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

3. Analysis  
 
3.1 Company Analysis 
During my internship at Forus, I had the opportunity to observe various key aspects of its 
operations, including productivity, operational capacity, and teamwork. 
Forus's mission is to be "Consumer Fanatic," focusing on the consumer. This means that 
everything the company does is designed with consumers in mind to provide them with the 
best experience and stand out in the industry. Since 2019, Forus has been working on a new 
strategy to become "phygital," combining digital and physical elements. To achieve this, they 
have been improving their e-commerce platforms to offer a seamless digital experience that 
complements their physical stores. This approach has led to significant improvements over 
the years. When the shift began, digital sales accounted for only 4%, but by 2023, they had 
grown to 26%, showing a big increase. 
The experience and talent of all the employees at Forus should be highlighted. Their 
professionalism and dedication to their work contribute to the company's productivity and 
efficiency. From my experience, I observed how different teams came together to deliver the 
best results and how different areas worked with each other for better ideas. This was 
evident in my case, as I had to attend multiple meetings with various brand managers and 
marketing professionals. 
Image 10: employees by range age 
Likewise, many employees were young, as can be seen in Image 10. A total of 1,877 people 
were under the age of 30 out of a total of 3,431, meaning that around 50% were young. 
The combination of youth with more experienced employees created a collaborative work 
environment, where there was an exchange of ideas and knowledge among workers. 
Furthermore, the company consistently provided training sessions and offered employees 
advanced programs and tools to facilitate their work. This left a strong impression on me, 
demonstrating how much the organization values and supports its team members. 
Taking all factors into account, Forus has shown remarkable growth and adaptability in 
various aspects, such as productivity, capacity, and customer satisfaction.  
 
 
 
 

3.2 Work Analysis 
When analyzing my work at Forus, it is evident that, despite the company's large scale, my 
contributions were significant in the area where I worked. I played a key role in helping on 
an important annual task which is the Annual Report. This document is fundamental to the 
company as it responds and presents key information about its performance each year. Its 
objective is to provide a comprehensive overview of the organization, including strategies, 
risk factors, sustainability impacts, and other critical aspects.  Also the company's Annual 
Report is essential because it ensures transparency, allowing investors and other 
stakeholders to understand the organization's performance and strategies. Additionally, this 
document complies with legal requirements and regulations established by regulatory and 
international entities. It also serves as a strategic decision tool, providing concrete data that 
helps the company define its business direction. Furthermore, it communicates the 
company's sustainability advancements and social responsibility efforts, which are 
increasingly valued by investors in the market. 
In addition, I collaborated with different members of the company to create this document. 
As mentioned earlier, I had to meet with several of Forus' brands to consult about their 
progress in sustainability and social matters. Through this, I was able to meet many people 
within the company and enhance my social skills in the professional environment.  
To minimize delays in the Annual Report creation process, I propose creating an organized 
index for the report. This index would allow me to easily locate the specific brand 
information during meetings, making the review easier, quicker and ensuring that I could 
present the data efficiently without wasting time.  
One of the challenges I faced during the creation of the Annual Report was coordinating 
with different brand managers and departments. There were instances where data was 
delayed or lacked clarity, which slowed down the overall process. While I managed to meet 
deadlines, the inefficiency of data collection from certain departments could have been 
improved. 
On the other hand, creating the database that Francisca requested was significant for the 
company. Before its creation, the company lacked organization and didnt have a clear 
structure for managing information about their sustainable products, making it difficult to 
ensure traceability. With the REP Law potentially coming into effect in a few years, having 
this data is essential to comply with the law. My work helped structure the data related to 
sustainability and the impact of textile products, not only facilitating analysis but also 
preparing the company to meet future regulatory requirements efficiently. 
I made some mistakes in the database at the beginning with some Excel tools. Initially, I 
wasn't efficient in gathering the attributes for each brand. However, as I mentioned before, I 
found a solution by discovering different tools that allowed me to be more efficient when 
searching for these terms. Also, with such a large database, it was easy to make mistakes, 
so I needed to stay fully focused during the creation process to avoid errors. 
 
 

I helped ensure the Annual Report was finished on time, meeting the CMF Chile deadlines 
and making it available to stakeholders as planned. 
In conclusion, my internship at Forus allowed me to contribute to key projects like the 
Annual Report and the sustainability database. I improved my technical skills, strengthened 
my communication, and gained insight into corporate sustainability. Despite challenges, I 
ensured efficiency in data management and reporting, making a lasting impact while 
growing professionally. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
​
​
 
 

4. Personal Reflection  
Looking back on my personal experience, this internship has been incredibly valuable for my 
professional and personal development. I was very nervous to start the internship since I 
didn’t have any experience working in a big company like Forus. I always felt that perhaps 
the things I had learned in university wouldn’t be very useful in practice, but after doing this 
work, my perception changed completely. This opportunity helped me clear up all my 
doubts, face new challenges, and make me have a big personal growth in all aspects. 
In the social aspect, collaborating with different members of the company was crucial to my 
personal growth. I was able to integrate into the team quickly and build strong working 
relationships. This made me analyze the importance of interpersonal relationships in 
business and how important they are for efficient work. All members of Forus were very 
supportive and always had a positive attitude toward helping me, which made me very 
grateful for the opportunity to join this internship. 
Being in projects such as the Annual Report, which is a critical document for many 
companies, not only taught me to handle sensitive information but also helped me to 
understand how these documents are important to the business context. This task makes 
me learn how to engage with different professionals from different backgrounds, improving 
my communication and teamwork skills. I also learned about the understanding of the 
importance of deadlines and how it is necessary to manage my time in an effective way.  
Moreover, being part of a large company like Forus, with its various departments, allowed 
me to appreciate the value of adaptability and learn from diverse perspectives. I learned to 
navigate different work styles and understand how each department’s contribution impacts 
the larger goals of the company. This helped me improve my problem-solving skills and 
adapt to changing circumstances with more flexibility. 
Additionally, as I worked on various assignments, I often had to find creative solutions to 
overcome challenges. This experience taught me how to handle pressure and prioritize tasks 
effectively. 
In conclusion, my internship at Forus allowed me to grow both professionally and personally. 
This experience boosted my confidence in my abilities and significantly improved my 
communication and teamwork skills. Additionally, I gained invaluable knowledge by working 
in a large organization. The challenges I faced and the lessons I learned throughout this 
program have better prepared me for my future career. I am truly grateful for the 
opportunity to have been part of the Forus team.

However, in some cases, the sustainability reports had not yet been published, as each 
company has its own reporting schedule. In these situations, it was necessary to 
communicate directly with the brands to obtain updated information for the 2024 fiscal year. 
As part of this task, I drafted emails in English and coordinated meetings with the managers 
of each brand, to validate and complement the data. These meetings also provided key 
information on initiatives that were more specific and couldn't be found in sustainability 
reports. 
Additionally, during my internship, I participated in weekly meetings with external agents 
(Kyklos) specialized in the REP Law. The objective of these meetings was to improve the 
automation of the information required to comply with the packaging regulation. Previously, 
the company was required to submit information on the REP Law once a year, but now it 
must do so monthly. Due to this change in reporting frequency, the company is seeking to 
automate the process. 
On the other hand, a few days before Christmas, the interns were called in to support the 
Forus stores at Alto Las Condes Mall. This experience allowed me to gain a deeper 
understanding of how the brands operate, particularly in terms of sales, inventory 
management, and distribution. Additionally, I had the opportunity to observe how the REP 
Law is implemented in malls, particularly in relation to the waste management of boxes. 
Lastly, during the final weeks of my internship, I focused on creating an extensive database 
for Francisca. This database consisted of collecting and organizing information from the 
catalogs of footwear, apparel, and accessories for Summer 2024 from all Forus brands. 
My main task was classifying sustainable products, which involved determining the 
sustainable attributes that could be assigned to each brand. For example, in the case of the 
Merrell brand, some footwear contained recycled polyester, which allowed them to be 
classified within the sustainable product category. To do this, it was necessary to analyze the 
specifications of each product in detail and structure the information in a way that made it 
easy to identify sustainability attributes within the catalog.

most_challenge_activity.texto:
2.2 Most Challenge Activity 
 
The most challenging task at Forus was creating a database to classify the sustainable 
attributes of each brand. This was particularly demanding due to my limited familiarity with 
advanced Excel tools. Consequently, I dedicated time to researching and exploring various 
functions to improve my skills, ultimately achieving an efficient and precise classification of 
the attributes assigned to each brand. 
 
The creation of this database was as follows: I individually downloaded the product catalogs 
from each brand and then consolidated them into a single Excel file to generate a 
comprehensive database for the company. 
 
The database contains approximately 7,600 SKUs (unique product identification codes) 
corresponding to the summer season of 2024, including all products sold in late 2024 and 
early 2025. In total, this excel had 109 columns, where each one represents different 
attributes of the product, such as brand, code, model, class, season, among others. 
 
Image 5: Screenshot database  
The main issue of this task and the reason it was assigned was the lack of accurate 
classification of sustainability products within the product catalog of each brand. As I 
mentioned earlier, this catalog contained 109 columns, being one of these columns called 
“Sustainable product”. A few brands completed this column correctly, and in many other 
cases, the information admitted was exactly or incomplete as it can be appreciated in image 
5.  
The lack of proper categorization impedes the identification and analysis of products with 
sustainable attributes, requiring a more detailed review and classification process. In 
addition, it is anticipated that in the coming years, Chile will include textiles as a priority 
product under the REP Law, which will require companies to implement waste management 
systems and take responsibility for the entire lifecycle of their products.  
The idea of creating this database was to make dynamic tables in Excel, which will help 
Francisca organize and analyze the data more easily in the future. These dynamic tables will 
allow her to filter and summarize the information based on the sustainable attributes of the 
products, making the analysis process much more efficient.  
Continuing, I developed the methodology for classifying attributes in different steps. First, I 
added two new columns to the database: “Attributes”, where I specified the characteristics 
 

that considered a product sustainable, and “Master Column”, which indicated the specific 
Excel column containing the relevant information. This structure facilitated efficient 
organization and retrieval of data pertinent to the classification of sustainable products. 
Image 6: New columns added 
Then, to classify which attribute would be considered, I began to realize a deep analysis of 
each brand individually. Over time, I created a “dictionary” of sustainable terms, allowing me 
to automate and organize the product classification process, as illustrated in the following 
image: 
Image 7: Dictionary of sustainable terms 
I created this dictionary to organize all the attributes, making them easy to find. In the first 
column, the brands are listed, the second column contains the assigned attribute, and the 
third column indicates in which catalog column the attribute can be found. 
To identify these terms, I utilized various Excel tools. Initially, I made numerous mistakes 
due to my limited knowledge of the software. However, through trial and error, I gradually 
optimized the process.  
 

Utilizing Excel tools such as Text to Columns, Conditional Formatting, and Advanced Filters 
simplified the process of identifying attributes within the 7,600 SKU, improving the efficiency 
of my research. 
Image 8: Conditional Formatting 
In image 8, it can be seen how the tool of conditional formatting works. This tool highlights 
cells containing a specific word; for example, in the illustration, it highlights if it says 
“recycled”. I used keywords related to sustainability, such as "recycled," "organic," "certified 
leather," "ecological.", among others. I also incorporated brand specific terms like Bluesign 
(a sustainability certification) or Repreve (a recycled polyester fiber made from used plastic 
bottles). 
After completing the dictionary, I utilized this tool to navigate to the two new columns I had 
created in the database. I organized the data by brand and systematically input information 
for each sustainable product. For instance, in Image 6, if a product featured a "100% 
recycled polyester lining," this attribute was classified as sustainable, and the corresponding 
column in the Excel sheet where this information could be found was indicated. To do this 
task I used Advanced Filters to be more organized.  
One of the main challenges I faced was that, on some occasions, the same product had 
multiple sustainable attributes. For example, a shoe was made from recycled fibers and also 
had a sole made from BLOOM, a material derived from algae. Since the purpose of this 
database is to analyze the data using dynamic tables, it was not possible to write both 
"recycled fibers" and "BLOOM sole" in the same cell. Doing so would have messed up the 
organization and stopped the dynamic tables from working correctly. Because of this, 
whenever I faced this issue, I had to create a new row in the database. I would input all the 
product information again, only changing the attribute and the master column. For example, 
in Image 9, it can be seen a SKU with different attributes. While the product code and all 
other information remain the same, the only change is in the columns I created. 
Image 9: Same product with different attributes 
 

This task taught me the importance of attention to detail because to complete this task 
effectively, it was crucial to maintain full concentration at all times. Given the large volume 
of rows and columns in the database, it was very easy to make mistakes, such as 
accidentally adding or deleting a row, which could have led to errors in the entire dataset. 
The sheer size of the database meant that even a small oversight could affect the accuracy 
of the information, making attention to detail and careful organization essential throughout 
the process. 
In conclusion, my work on the database gave Forus a solid foundation to manage its 
sustainable products effectively. This will not only help the company stay organized but also 
prepare for future regulations such as the REP Law. By accurately tracking sustainable 
attributes, Forus can meet the legal requirements and maintain transparency with 
consumers and stakeholders. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

3. Analysis

company_analysis.texto:
3.1 Company Analysis 
During my internship at Forus, I had the opportunity to observe various key aspects of its 
operations, including productivity, operational capacity, and teamwork. 
Forus's mission is to be "Consumer Fanatic," focusing on the consumer. This means that 
everything the company does is designed with consumers in mind to provide them with the 
best experience and stand out in the industry. Since 2019, Forus has been working on a new 
strategy to become "phygital," combining digital and physical elements. To achieve this, they 
have been improving their e-commerce platforms to offer a seamless digital experience that 
complements their physical stores. This approach has led to significant improvements over 
the years. When the shift began, digital sales accounted for only 4%, but by 2023, they had 
grown to 26%, showing a big increase. 
The experience and talent of all the employees at Forus should be highlighted. Their 
professionalism and dedication to their work contribute to the company's productivity and 
efficiency. From my experience, I observed how different teams came together to deliver the 
best results and how different areas worked with each other for better ideas. This was 
evident in my case, as I had to attend multiple meetings with various brand managers and 
marketing professionals. 
Image 10: employees by range age 
Likewise, many employees were young, as can be seen in Image 10. A total of 1,877 people 
were under the age of 30 out of a total of 3,431, meaning that around 50% were young. 
The combination of youth with more experienced employees created a collaborative work 
environment, where there was an exchange of ideas and knowledge among workers. 
Furthermore, the company consistently provided training sessions and offered employees 
advanced programs and tools to facilitate their work. This left a strong impression on me, 
demonstrating how much the organization values and supports its team members. 
Taking all factors into account, Forus has shown remarkable growth and adaptability in 
various aspects, such as productivity, capacity, and customer satisfaction.

work_analysis.texto:
3.2 Work Analysis 
When analyzing my work at Forus, it is evident that, despite the company's large scale, my 
contributions were significant in the area where I worked. I played a key role in helping on 
an important annual task which is the Annual Report. This document is fundamental to the 
company as it responds and presents key information about its performance each year. Its 
objective is to provide a comprehensive overview of the organization, including strategies, 
risk factors, sustainability impacts, and other critical aspects.  Also the company's Annual 
Report is essential because it ensures transparency, allowing investors and other 
stakeholders to understand the organization's performance and strategies. Additionally, this 
document complies with legal requirements and regulations established by regulatory and 
international entities. It also serves as a strategic decision tool, providing concrete data that 
helps the company define its business direction. Furthermore, it communicates the 
company's sustainability advancements and social responsibility efforts, which are 
increasingly valued by investors in the market. 
In addition, I collaborated with different members of the company to create this document. 
As mentioned earlier, I had to meet with several of Forus' brands to consult about their 
progress in sustainability and social matters. Through this, I was able to meet many people 
within the company and enhance my social skills in the professional environment.  
To minimize delays in the Annual Report creation process, I propose creating an organized 
index for the report. This index would allow me to easily locate the specific brand 
information during meetings, making the review easier, quicker and ensuring that I could 
present the data efficiently without wasting time.  
One of the challenges I faced during the creation of the Annual Report was coordinating 
with different brand managers and departments. There were instances where data was 
delayed or lacked clarity, which slowed down the overall process. While I managed to meet 
deadlines, the inefficiency of data collection from certain departments could have been 
improved. 
On the other hand, creating the database that Francisca requested was significant for the 
company. Before its creation, the company lacked organization and didnt have a clear 
structure for managing information about their sustainable products, making it difficult to 
ensure traceability. With the REP Law potentially coming into effect in a few years, having 
this data is essential to comply with the law. My work helped structure the data related to 
sustainability and the impact of textile products, not only facilitating analysis but also 
preparing the company to meet future regulatory requirements efficiently. 
I made some mistakes in the database at the beginning with some Excel tools. Initially, I 
wasn't efficient in gathering the attributes for each brand. However, as I mentioned before, I 
found a solution by discovering different tools that allowed me to be more efficient when 
searching for these terms. Also, with such a large database, it was easy to make mistakes, 
so I needed to stay fully focused during the creation process to avoid errors. 
 
 

I helped ensure the Annual Report was finished on time, meeting the CMF Chile deadlines 
and making it available to stakeholders as planned. 
In conclusion, my internship at Forus allowed me to contribute to key projects like the 
Annual Report and the sustainability database. I improved my technical skills, strengthened 
my communication, and gained insight into corporate sustainability. Despite challenges, I 
ensured efficiency in data management and reporting, making a lasting impact while 
growing professionally. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
​
​
 
 

4.

personal_reflection.texto:
Personal Reflection  
Looking back on my personal experience, this internship has been incredibly valuable for my 
professional and personal development. I was very nervous to start the internship since I 
didn’t have any experience working in a big company like Forus. I always felt that perhaps 
the things I had learned in university wouldn’t be very useful in practice, but after doing this 
work, my perception changed completely. This opportunity helped me clear up all my 
doubts, face new challenges, and make me have a big personal growth in all aspects. 
In the social aspect, collaborating with different members of the company was crucial to my 
personal growth. I was able to integrate into the team quickly and build strong working 
relationships. This made me analyze the importance of interpersonal relationships in 
business and how important they are for efficient work. All members of Forus were very 
supportive and always had a positive attitude toward helping me, which made me very 
grateful for the opportunity to join this internship. 
Being in projects such as the Annual Report, which is a critical document for many 
companies, not only taught me to handle sensitive information but also helped me to 
understand how these documents are important to the business context. This task makes 
me learn how to engage with different professionals from different backgrounds, improving 
my communication and teamwork skills. I also learned about the understanding of the 
importance of deadlines and how it is necessary to manage my time in an effective way.  
Moreover, being part of a large company like Forus, with its various departments, allowed 
me to appreciate the value of adaptability and learn from diverse perspectives. I learned to 
navigate different work styles and understand how each department’s contribution impacts 
the larger goals of the company. This helped me improve my problem-solving skills and 
adapt to changing circumstances with more flexibility. 
Additionally, as I worked on various assignments, I often had to find creative solutions to 
overcome challenges. This experience taught me how to handle pressure and prioritize tasks 
effectively. 
In conclusion, my internship at Forus allowed me to grow both professionally and personally. 
This experience boosted my confidence in my abilities and significantly improved my 
communication and teamwork skills. Additionally, I gained invaluable knowledge by working 
in a large organization. The challenges I faced and the lessons I learned throughout this 
program have better prepared me for my future career. I am truly grateful for the 
opportunity to have been part of the Forus team.

There were instances where data was  delayed or lacked clarity, which slowed down the overall process.

While I managed to meet  deadlines, the inefficiency of data collection from certain departments could have been  improved. On the other hand, creating the database that Francisca requested was significant for the  company. Before its creation, the company lacked organization and didnt have a clear  structure for managing information about their sustainable products, making it difficult to  ensure traceability.

With the REP Law potentially coming into effect in a few years, having  this data is essential to comply with the law. My work helped structure the data related to  sustainability and the impact of textile products, not only facilitating analysis but also  preparing the company to meet future regulatory requirements efficiently. I made some mistakes in the database at the beginning with some Excel tools. Initially, I  wasn't efficient in gathering the attributes for each brand.

However, as I mentioned before, I  found a solution by discovering different tools that allowed me to be more efficient when  searching for these terms. Also, with such a large database, it was easy to make mistakes,  so I needed to stay fully focused during the creation process to avoid errors. I helped ensure the Annual Report was finished on time, meeting the CMF Chile deadlines  and making it available to stakeholders as planned.

In conclusion, my internship at Forus allowed me to contribute to key projects like the  Annual Report and the sustainability database. I improved my technical skills, strengthened  my communication, and gained insight into corporate sustainability. Despite challenges, I  ensured efficiency in data management and reporting, making a lasting impact while  growing professionally. ​ ​      4.

Personal Reflection   Looking back on my personal experience, this internship has been incredibly valuable for my  professional and personal development. I was very nervous to start the internship since I  didn’t have any experience working in a big company like Forus. I always felt that perhaps  the things I had learned in university wouldn’t be very useful in practice, but after doing this  work, my perception changed completely.

This opportunity helped me clear up all my  doubts, face new challenges, and make me have a big personal growth in all aspects. In the social aspect, collaborating with different members of the company was crucial to my  personal growth. I was able to integrate into the team quickly and build strong working  relationships. This made me analyze the importance of interpersonal relationships in  business and how important they are for efficient work.

All members of Forus were very  supportive and always had a positive attitude toward helping me, which made me very  grateful for the opportunity to join this internship. Being in projects such as the Annual Report, which is a critical document for many  companies, not only taught me to handle sensitive information but also helped me to  understand how these documents are important to the business context.

This task makes  me learn how to engage with different professionals from different backgrounds, improving  my communication and teamwork skills. I also learned about the understanding of the  importance of deadlines and how it is necessary to manage my time in an effective way. Moreover, being part of a large company like Forus, with its various departments, allowed  me to appreciate the value of adaptability and learn from diverse perspectives.

I learned to  navigate different work styles and understand how each department’s contribution impacts  the larger goals of the company. This helped me improve my problem-solving skills and  adapt to changing circumstances with more flexibility. Additionally, as I worked on various assignments, I often had to find creative solutions to  overcome challenges. This experience taught me how to handle pressure and prioritize tasks  effectively.

In conclusion, my internship at Forus allowed me to grow both professionally and personally. This experience boosted my confidence in my abilities and significantly improved my  communication and teamwork skills. Additionally, I gained invaluable knowledge by working  in a large organization. The challenges I faced and the lessons I learned throughout this  program have better prepared me for my future career. I am truly grateful for the  opportunity to have been part of the Forus team.

embeddings_data.texto: Abstract  During my internship at Forus S.A., I worked in the Sustainability Sub-Management under  the supervision of Francisca Menichetti Pilasi. My main responsibilities included contributing  to the 2024 Annual Report, focusing on sustainability, and developing a database to classify  sustainable product attributes across the company’s brand portfolio.

The Annual Report task required extensive research, data collection, and coordination with  brand managers to ensure accurate reporting of sustainability initiatives. I also participated  in meetings with external agents to optimize compliance with the Extended Producer  Responsibility (REP) Law, reinforcing Forus’s commitment to environmental sustainability. One of the biggest challenges was building a database to identify sustainable products.

This  process involved organizing more than 7,600 SKUs and 109 columns in Excel, using dynamic  tables, conditional formatting, and advanced filters. This tool will facilitate the tracking of  sustainable products and help Forus adapt to future regulations. Additionally, the internship allowed me to gain insights into retail operations, from in-store  activities to sustainability management.

I collaborated with Investor Relations and  Sustainability teams, strengthening my communication and project management skills. The internship experience was essential for my professional growth, enhancing my technical  skills, fostering personal development, and providing valuable insights into the operations of  a large company. 1. Introduction.

1.1 Company Description     Forus S.A. is a company in the retail sector that specialize in footwear, clothing, and  accessories for women, men, youth, and children. It was founded in August 1980 and began  its operations as Hush Puppies Chile Ltda. Through the years, the brand portfolio and  operations of the organization have expanded, building a strong position in the design,  development, and distribution of footwear, apparel, and accessories.

Currently Forus is  operating in four countries: Chile, Peru, Colombia, and Uruguay, successfully integrating  both international and in-house brands into its portfolio. This organization distributes 28 prestigious international and proprietary brands, including  Columbia, Under Armour, Vans, Hush Puppies, Rockford, among others. These brands cover  multiple sections including outdoor, action sports, athletic wear, lifestyle, and safety  footwear.

The company operates under a phygital ecosystem that effectively integrates  physical stores, proprietary e-commerce websites, and marketplaces. This approach ensures  a superior customer experience across all channels.

Image 1: General diagram of the company  This image shows the company's structure, which is led by the Board of Directors and  General Management, with key areas such as Finance, Sustainability, Logistics, and Digital,  as well as regional management in Colombia, Uruguay, and Peru. In addition, there is a  close relationship between the different areas of the company, which is essential for  optimizing operational efficiency and fostering collaboration among teams.

The company's long journey has led to an organization with 3,421 employees. This large  team reflects Forus commitment to growth and continuous development. Furthermore, total  sales in 2023 reached 326,555 million pesos, supported by a strong network of sales  channels that includes 399 physical stores, 46 e-commerce websites, and participation in 13  marketplaces.

1.2 Area Description   In 2023, Forus created a Sustainability Sub Management, led by my supervisor, Francisca  Menichetti Pilasi. The mission of this area is to identify the environmental, social, and  governance (ESG) issues that have the greatest impact on the company, its stakeholders,  and the environment, with the goal of focusing efforts and resources on initiatives that allow  for progress and the monitoring of these key aspects.

The Sustainability department integrates sustainable practices into all operations of the  company, standing out for implementing circular economy projects and waste management,  such as the “Paperless” program and compliance with the REP law (Extended Producer  Responsibility), where I worked on some projects related to it.

It also develops sustainability  indicators for products and leads initiatives like 'For the Community,' promoting corporate  volunteering and activities with a positive social and environmental impact  This department reports directly to the Legal and People Management, which allows it to  address sustainability issues from a corporate perspective and work on them strategically.

Additionally, it collaborates closely with the Investor Relations Management, ensuring that  sustainability initiatives align with stakeholder expectations and that progress on ESG  matters is effectively communicated. During my internship, I participated in various projects, the most important being the  preparation of the company's Annual Report 2024, with a special focus on the sustainability  section.

To develop this task, it was necessary to do a lot of research and also coordinate  meetings with all the company’s brands, which allowed me to work directly with the brand  managers to gather information about their sustainable initiatives. Additionally, I worked closely with Martina Avsolomovich, an intern of Isabel Darrigrandi,  Head of Investor Relations (as Francisca and Isabel were responsible for drafting the  document of the Annual Report).

I also developed an extensive database to classify and  track sustainable attributes across all the brand's products. This task allowed a more  comprehensive understanding of their sustainable offerings, helping the company to identify  environmentally friendly products more effectively. Image 2: Sustainability Department diagram  My supervisor, Francisca Menichetti, had a direct relationship with me since she is the  Sub-Manager and she is the only one in the department.
She works closely with her intern,  which allowed me to learn about various topics, especially sustainability strategies in a retail  company and interaction with other departments. 2. Work Details.

2.1 General Activities  As mentioned earlier, a large part of my work was focused on gathering information for the  2024 Annual Report, specifically in the sustainability section. To carry out this task, it was  first necessary to collect and analyze a significant amount of information from the  sustainability reports of the various Forus brands.

The first days of my internship were dedicated to reading and analyzing the company’s 2023  Annual Report, which involved several hours of work and research. This task was essential  as it allowed me to familiarize myself with environmental concepts I was unfamiliar with and  gain a deeper understanding of the structure of Chapter 6, titled "Our Planet and  Communities," which was the chapter I had to develop for the 2024 Annual Report.

Image 3: Organization of Annual Report document    To begin this task, documents were created for each chapter of the report so they could be  updated. As I mentioned earlier, I worked on chapter 6 and organized the document, as can  be seen in image 3. Image 4: Document Structure  Then I started gathering information for the 2024 Annual Report, where I created a  well-structured document with tables for each brand, featuring two main columns: 2023 and     2024.

In the first column, I wrote the information available in the 2023 Annual Report, while  in the second, I added only the relevant updates for each topic. This required extensive  research on the various brands in the Forus portfolio, with a big focus on Crocs and  L'Occitane, as they were incorporated that same year and lacked prior information in the  report.

For brands like Columbia, Rockford, VF Company, and Under Armour, the task was to  update the existing data, as can be seen in the image 4  However, in some cases, the sustainability reports had not yet been published, as each  company has its own reporting schedule. In these situations, it was necessary to  communicate directly with the brands to obtain updated information for the 2024 fiscal year.

As part of this task, I drafted emails in English and coordinated meetings with the managers  of each brand, to validate and complement the data. These meetings also provided key  information on initiatives that were more specific and couldn't be found in sustainability  reports. Additionally, during my internship, I participated in weekly meetings with external agents  (Kyklos) specialized in the REP Law.

The objective of these meetings was to improve the  automation of the information required to comply with the packaging regulation. Previously,  the company was required to submit information on the REP Law once a year, but now it  must do so monthly. Due to this change in reporting frequency, the company is seeking to  automate the process. On the other hand, a few days before Christmas, the interns were called in to support the  Forus stores at Alto Las Condes Mall.

This experience allowed me to gain a deeper  understanding of how the brands operate, particularly in terms of sales, inventory  management, and distribution. Additionally, I had the opportunity to observe how the REP  Law is implemented in malls, particularly in relation to the waste management of boxes. Lastly, during the final weeks of my internship, I focused on creating an extensive database  for Francisca.

This database consisted of collecting and organizing information from the  catalogs of footwear, apparel, and accessories for Summer 2024 from all Forus brands. My main task was classifying sustainable products, which involved determining the  sustainable attributes that could be assigned to each brand. For example, in the case of the  Merrell brand, some footwear contained recycled polyester, which allowed them to be  classified within the sustainable product category.

To do this, it was necessary to analyze the  specifications of each product in detail and structure the information in a way that made it  easy to identify sustainability attributes within the catalog.

2.2 Most Challenge Activity    The most challenging task at Forus was creating a database to classify the sustainable  attributes of each brand. This was particularly demanding due to my limited familiarity with  advanced Excel tools. Consequently, I dedicated time to researching and exploring various  functions to improve my skills, ultimately achieving an efficient and precise classification of  the attributes assigned to each brand.

The creation of this database was as follows: I individually downloaded the product catalogs  from each brand and then consolidated them into a single Excel file to generate a  comprehensive database for the company. The database contains approximately 7,600 SKUs (unique product identification codes)  corresponding to the summer season of 2024, including all products sold in late 2024 and  early 2025.

In total, this excel had 109 columns, where each one represents different  attributes of the product, such as brand, code, model, class, season, among others. Image 5: Screenshot database   The main issue of this task and the reason it was assigned was the lack of accurate  classification of sustainability products within the product catalog of each brand. As I  mentioned earlier, this catalog contained 109 columns, being one of these columns called  “Sustainable product”.

A few brands completed this column correctly, and in many other  cases, the information admitted was exactly or incomplete as it can be appreciated in image  5. The lack of proper categorization impedes the identification and analysis of products with  sustainable attributes, requiring a more detailed review and classification process.

In  addition, it is anticipated that in the coming years, Chile will include textiles as a priority  product under the REP Law, which will require companies to implement waste management  systems and take responsibility for the entire lifecycle of their products. The idea of creating this database was to make dynamic tables in Excel, which will help  Francisca organize and analyze the data more easily in the future.

These dynamic tables will  allow her to filter and summarize the information based on the sustainable attributes of the  products, making the analysis process much more efficient. Continuing, I developed the methodology for classifying attributes in different steps.

First, I  added two new columns to the database: “Attributes”, where I specified the characteristics     that considered a product sustainable, and “Master Column”, which indicated the specific  Excel column containing the relevant information. This structure facilitated efficient  organization and retrieval of data pertinent to the classification of sustainable products.

Image 6: New columns added  Then, to classify which attribute would be considered, I began to realize a deep analysis of  each brand individually. Over time, I created a “dictionary” of sustainable terms, allowing me  to automate and organize the product classification process, as illustrated in the following  image:  Image 7: Dictionary of sustainable terms  I created this dictionary to organize all the attributes, making them easy to find.

In the first  column, the brands are listed, the second column contains the assigned attribute, and the  third column indicates in which catalog column the attribute can be found. To identify these terms, I utilized various Excel tools. Initially, I made numerous mistakes  due to my limited knowledge of the software. However, through trial and error, I gradually  optimized the process.

Utilizing Excel tools such as Text to Columns, Conditional Formatting, and Advanced Filters  simplified the process of identifying attributes within the 7,600 SKU, improving the efficiency  of my research. Image 8: Conditional Formatting  In image 8, it can be seen how the tool of conditional formatting works. This tool highlights  cells containing a specific word; for example, in the illustration, it highlights if it says  “recycled”.

I used keywords related to sustainability, such as "recycled," "organic," "certified  leather," "ecological.", among others. I also incorporated brand specific terms like Bluesign  (a sustainability certification) or Repreve (a recycled polyester fiber made from used plastic  bottles). After completing the dictionary, I utilized this tool to navigate to the two new columns I had  created in the database.

I organized the data by brand and systematically input information  for each sustainable product. For instance, in Image 6, if a product featured a "100%  recycled polyester lining," this attribute was classified as sustainable, and the corresponding  column in the Excel sheet where this information could be found was indicated. To do this  task I used Advanced Filters to be more organized.

One of the main challenges I faced was that, on some occasions, the same product had  multiple sustainable attributes. For example, a shoe was made from recycled fibers and also  had a sole made from BLOOM, a material derived from algae. Since the purpose of this  database is to analyze the data using dynamic tables, it was not possible to write both  "recycled fibers" and "BLOOM sole" in the same cell.

Doing so would have messed up the  organization and stopped the dynamic tables from working correctly. Because of this,  whenever I faced this issue, I had to create a new row in the database. I would input all the  product information again, only changing the attribute and the master column. For example,  in Image 9, it can be seen a SKU with different attributes. While the product code and all  other information remain the same, the only change is in the columns I created.

Image 9: Same product with different attributes     This task taught me the importance of attention to detail because to complete this task  effectively, it was crucial to maintain full concentration at all times. Given the large volume  of rows and columns in the database, it was very easy to make mistakes, such as  accidentally adding or deleting a row, which could have led to errors in the entire dataset.

The sheer size of the database meant that even a small oversight could affect the accuracy  of the information, making attention to detail and careful organization essential throughout  the process. In conclusion, my work on the database gave Forus a solid foundation to manage its  sustainable products effectively. This will not only help the company stay organized but also  prepare for future regulations such as the REP Law.

evidencia_recuperada.texto: For brands like Columbia, Rockford, VF Company, and Under Armour, the task was to  update the existing data, as can be seen in the image 4  However, in some cases, the sustainability reports had not yet been published, as each  company has its own reporting schedule. In these situations, it was necessary to  communicate directly with the brands to obtain updated information for the 2024 fiscal year.

This task makes  me learn how to engage with different professionals from different backgrounds, improving  my communication and teamwork skills. I also learned about the understanding of the  importance of deadlines and how it is necessary to manage my time in an effective way. Moreover, being part of a large company like Forus, with its various departments, allowed  me to appreciate the value of adaptability and learn from diverse perspectives.

Abstract  During my internship at Forus S.A., I worked in the Sustainability Sub-Management under  the supervision of Francisca Menichetti Pilasi. My main responsibilities included contributing  to the 2024 Annual Report, focusing on sustainability, and developing a database to classify  sustainable product attributes across the company’s brand portfolio.
